chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P No.D-276 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 30.04.2025 Mr. Prem Chand advocate a/w petitioner No.1 Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi Assistant A.G a/w IO Inspector Ghulam Mustafa <>*<> Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that petitioner No.1 was earlier married with respondent No.4, who pronounced divorce to her and after observing Iddat period she has now contracted second marriage with petitioner No.2, and being annoyed by such marriage the respondent No.4 has lodged false FIR bearing Crime No.07 of 2025 at P.S Boder Farm District Umerkot under Sections 365-B, 364-A, 452, 34 PPC read with Section 3 and 2 of Preventions of Trafficking in Persons Act 2018. Investigation Officer has recorded the statement of petitioner No.1, wherein she has categorically stated that she has contracted second marriage with petitioner No.2 out of her own free will and accord and no one has abducted her. Investigation Officer submits that based on the statement of petitioner No.1 he will file his report under Section 173 Cr.P.C before the concerned Magistrate for disposal of the case in accordance with law. Counsel for the petitioners expressed satisfaction and seeks disposal of the petition. In view of the above, instant petition is disposed of with directions to Investigation Officer to file his report before the learned Magistrate concerned keeping in view of the statement of petitioner No.1/alleged abducted recorded under Section 161 Cr.P.C within a week’s time for disposal of the case in accordance with law. Meanwhile, petitioners be provided protection in accordance with law.

In view of the above, instant petition is disposed of with directions to Investigation Officer to file his report before the learned Magistrate concerned keeping in view of the statement of petitioner No.1/alleged abducted recorded under Section 161 Cr.P.C within a week’s time for disposal of the case in accordance with law. Meanwhile, petitioners be provided protection in accordance with law. JUDGE JUDGE *Sajjad*
